ogTitle: James Well vs Jamieson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in James Well and Jamieson.
heading: James Well vs Jamieson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between James Well, South Australia and Jamieson, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jamieson has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to James Well (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is James Well or Jamieson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: James Well has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Jamieson scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: James Well scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Jamieson scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in James Well or Jamieson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jamieson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
